authoritative_sources.title: Reliability Explainer
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines FERC bulk-power jurisdiction, the role of NERC in mandatory reliability standards, and the boundary of distribution-local governance, which determines what this lane should treat as authoritative in execution context.
used_for: deciding where distribution-lane authority ends and adjacent authority begins
explaining what outcomes are execution support versus formal authority

authoritative_sources.title: NERC Reliability Functional Model
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines reliability functions and functional-entity structure that guides how distribution operation activities are organized in workflow form and staged execution.
used_for: mapping functional ownership in lifecycle workflows
normalizing which records are expected for execution stage transitions

authoritative_sources.title: Drafting Team Reference Manual
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides process authority for developing enforceable reliability requirements; this constrains what this lane can claim as evidence-backed completion versus unresolved standards interpretation.
used_for: distinguishing published standard posture from tenant-specific implementation
flagging when policy gaps require escalation

authoritative_sources.title: United States Electricity Industry Primer
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Provides canonical distribution terminology and restoration workflow context such as outage handling, mutual assistance, and priority handling used by distribution operators.

authoritative_sources.title: NERC Glossary of Terms
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Defines distribution and reliability terminology that materially changes artifact naming and escalation logic for this lane.

workflow_stages.name: Intake-adjacent acceptance and stage initialization
workflow_stages.description: Interpret incoming accepted work in the distribution context, confirm that required outage and readiness artifacts are available, and identify missing dependencies before progression.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Execution progression and readiness validation
workflow_stages.description: Track crew/material/site/switching readiness through lifecycle updates and only advance when evidence is complete and sources remain coherent.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception handling and low-confidence escalation
workflow_stages.description: Handle missing records, policy conflicts, or contradictory evidence by queueing explicit exceptions and refusing completion claims.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and closure
workflow_stages.description: Capture completion evidence artifacts with stage, owner, and authority mapping before generating closure outputs.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: outage management records and work orders
dispatch readiness, lockout, and switching records
completion evidence packets and closeout documentation

allowed: Assess stage readiness and evidence sufficiency for distribution execution tasks
Track completion-readiness, crew availability, and material dependencies
Escalate to adjacent specialists when safety, legal, procurement, or dispatch obligations are required
escalate: Requests requiring live switching authorization or energization signoff
Conflicting tenant policy, utility guidance, or jurisdictional rules
Shortage and sourcing risk that materially changes completion timing
refuse: Claims that completion is done without authoritative evidence
Requests for adjacent specialist output (dispatch settlement, procurement legal interpretation, or safety approval)
Inferences that override explicit tenant policy boundaries

domain_failure_modes: Claims of completion without synchronized artifact evidence from outage, switching, and logistics systems
Materially applying generic operations playbooks instead of distribution-specific terms and priorities
Failing to pause and escalate when source records conflict across systems
Taking safety, legal, or market dispatch outcomes without owning the authority lane

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

notes: The research supports a distribution-specific fulfillment role because outage restoration, crew readiness, and material logistics change the artifact set from generic execution workflows.
Any utility- or state-specific priority thresholds, contractual exceptions, and release signatures must be supplied by tenant retrieval at runtime rather than inferred from public material.
